debug_info.goal:
ι : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
m : MeasurableSpace Ω
X : Ω → Set ι
u : Set ι
i j : ι
p : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
hX : @ProbabilityTheory.IsBernoulliOn ι Ω m X u p P
hi : i ∈ u
hj : j ∈ u
⊢ map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P

      congr

      <;> simp [hi, hj]

    have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) =ᵐ[P] (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) := by rfl
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) =ᵐ[P] (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) := by rfl
    have h₃ : map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P := by
      have h₄ : map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | i ∈ s} := by
        have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ X ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
        have h₆ : map (fun ω ↦ X ω) P = Ber(u, p) := by
          rw [← h₅.1]
        calc
          map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ ω) (map (fun ω ↦ X ω) P) := by
            rw [map_map]
            <;> simp [Function.comp]
          _ = map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ ω) (Ber(u, p)) := by rw [h₆]
          _ = (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | i ∈ s} := by
            simp [Measure.restrict_apply, Filter.eventually_def, map_apply]
            <;> congr 1 <;> funext s <;> simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      have h₇ : map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | j ∈ s} := by
        have h₈ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ X ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
        have h₉ : map (fun ω ↦ X ω) P = Ber(u, p) := by
          rw [← h₈.1]
        calc
          map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ ω) (map (fun ω ↦ X ω) P) := by
            rw [map_map]
            <;> simp [Function.comp]
          _ = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ ω) (Ber(u, p)) := by rw [h₉]
          _ = (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | j ∈ s} := by
            simp [Measure.restrict_apply, Filter.eventually_def, map_apply]
            <;> congr 1 <;> funext s <;> simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      have h₁₀ : (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | i ∈ s} = (Ber(u, p)).restrict {s | j ∈ s} := by
        have h₁₁ : {s : Set ι | i ∈ s} = {s : Set ι | j ∈ s} := by
          ext s
          simp [hi, hj, iff_def]
          <;> tauto
        rw [h₁₁]
      rw [h₄, h₇, h₁₀]
    exact h₃

by
    have h₁ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ (fun ω => (X ω : Set ι)) ω) P := rfl
    have h₂ : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ (fun ω => (X ω : Set ι)) ω) P := rfl
    have h₃ : (map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P) = (map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P) := by
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of X is symmetric in i and j
      -- since the Bernoulli distribution is the same for each coordinate
      have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω => (X ω : Set ι)) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      have h₅ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) := by
        rw [← h₄.map]
        <;> simp [map_map]
      have h₆ : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
        rw [← h₄.map]
        <;> simp [map_map]
      rw [h₅, h₆]
      -- The distribution Ber(u, p) is symmetric in i and j
      -- because i and j are both in u and p is the same for each coordinate
      have h₇ : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
        -- Prove that the distribution of i ∈ s is the same as the distribution of j ∈ s
        -- This follows from the fact that the Bernoulli distribution is the same for each coordinate
        have h₈ : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) = (if i ∈ u then p else 0) := by sorry
        have h₉ : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) = (if j ∈ u then p else 0) := by sorry
        rw [h₈, h₉]
        <;> simp [hi, hj]
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₇]
    exact h₃

by
    have h1 : (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) =ᵐ[P] (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) := by rfl
    have h2 : (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) =ᵐ[P] (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) := by rfl
    have h3 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push {i}) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
      have h4 : HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P := hX
      have h5 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) P := by
        -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
        have h6 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) P := by
          -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
          refine' h4.map _
          -- Define the function that maps a set to whether i is in it
          exact measurableSetOf_mem
        exact h6
      -- Simplify the pushforward measure
      convert h5 using 1
      <;> simp [Set.map]
      <;> ext <;> simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> aesop
    have h4 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push {j}) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of j ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
      have h5 : HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P := hX
      have h6 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) P := by
        -- Use the fact that the distribution of j ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
        have h7 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) P := by
          -- Use the fact that the distribution of j ∈ X is the same as the distribution of X
          refine' h5.map _
          -- Define the function that maps a set to whether j is in it
          exact measurableSetOf_mem
        exact h7
      -- Simplify the pushforward measure
      convert h6 using 1
      <;> simp [Set.map]
      <;> ext <;> simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> aesop
    -- Show that the distributions are the same
    have h5 : Ber(u, p).push {i} = Ber(u, p).push {j} := by
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of j ∈ X
      have h6 : i ∈ u := hi
      have h7 : j ∈ u := hj
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of j ∈ X
      have h8 : Ber(u, p).push {i} = Ber(u, p).push {j} := by
        -- Use the fact that the distribution of i ∈ X is the same as the distribution of j ∈ X
        simp_all [bernoulliOn_eq_map, Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
        <;> aesop
      exact h8
    -- Combine all the results to show that the distributions are the same
    have h6 : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the distributions are the same
      have h7 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push {i}) P := h3
      have h8 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).push {j}) P := h4
      have h9 : Ber(u, p).push {i} = Ber(u, p).push {j} := h5
      -- Use the fact that the distributions are the same
      have h10 : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = Ber(u, p).push {i} := by
        -- Use the fact that the distributions are the same
        rw [← h7]
        <;> simp [hasLaw]
      have h11 : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = Ber(u, p).push {j} := by
        -- Use the fact that the distributions are the same
        rw [← h8]
        <;> simp [hasLaw]
      -- Combine all the results to show that the distributions are the same
      rw [h10, h11, h9]
    -- Use the fact that the distributions are the same
    exact h6

by
    have h1 : (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω).comp (Equiv.ofBijective (Equiv.swap i j) (by
        constructor <;>
        intro a b <;>
        simp_all [Equiv.swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne, hi, hj] <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all }) <;>
        (try { decide })
      )) = (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) := by
      funext ω
      simp [Equiv.swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne, hi, hj]
      <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try { simp_all }) <;>
      (try { decide })
    have h2 : map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P := by
      have h3 : HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P := hX
      have h4 : Law (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = Law (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
        have h5 : Law (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) := by
          rw [h3.map]
          <;> simp [Measure.map_map]
          <;> congr
          <;> ext
          <;> simp [Set.mem_image]
        have h6 : Law (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
          rw [h3.map]
          <;> simp [Measure.map_map]
          <;> congr
          <;> ext
          <;> simp [Set.mem_image]
        rw [h5, h6]
        have h7 : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) = (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
          apply Measure.ext
          intro s
          have h8 : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => i ∈ s) s = Ber(u, p) ((fun s => i ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) := by
            simp [Measure.map_apply]
          have h9 : (Ber(u, p)).map (fun s => j ∈ s) s = Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) := by
            simp [Measure.map_apply]
          rw [h8, h9]
          have h10 : Ber(u, p) ((fun s => i ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) = Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) := by
            -- Use the fact that the measures are the same when considering permutations
            have h11 : (
              (fun s => i ∈ s) ⁻¹' s = (fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹'
                (Equiv.swap i j ⁻¹' s)) := by
              ext
              simp [Equiv.swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne, hi, hj]
              <;>
              (try { aesop }) <;>
              (try { simp_all }) <;>
              (try { decide })
            rw [h11]
            have h12 : Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' (Equiv.swap i j ⁻¹' s)) =
              Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) := by
              -- Apply the permutation symmetry of the Bernoulli measure
              have h13 : MeasurableEquiv.SetLike (Equiv.swap i j) (Set ι) :=
                {
                  toFun := (Equiv.swap i j)
                  invFun := (Equiv.swap i j).symm
                  left_inv := by
                    intro x
                    simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
                  right_inv := by
                    intro x
                    simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
                  measurable := by infer_instance
                  measurable_inv := by infer_instance
                  isEquiv := by infer_instance
                }
              have h14 : Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' (Equiv.swap i j ⁻¹' s)) =
                  Ber(u, p) ((fun s => j ∈ s) ⁻¹' s) := by
                rw [← h13.congr]
                congr
                <;>
                (try { simp [Equiv.swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne, hi, hj] }) <;>
                (try { aesop }) <;>
                (try { simp_all }) <;>
                (try { decide })
              rw [h14]
            rw [h12]
          rw [h10]
        rw [h7]
      have h8 : Law (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = Law (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := h4
      have h9 : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
        simpa [Law] using h8
      exact h9
    exact h2

by
    have h₁ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => (i ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) := by
      have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω => X ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of X is the product Bernoulli measure on u with parameter p
      -- to deduce that the distribution of (i ∈ X ω) is the same as (i ∈ u)
      sorry
    have h₂ : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => (j ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) := by
      have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω => X ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      -- Similarly for j ∈ u
      sorry
    -- Since i ∈ u and j ∈ u, both (i ∈ u) and (j ∈ u) are true, so their distributions are the same
    have h₃ : i ∈ u := hi
    have h₄ : j ∈ u := hj
    have h₅ : map (fun ω => (i ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) = map (fun ω => (j ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the product Bernoulli measure on u is symmetric for all elements in u
      sorry
    -- Combine the above results to conclude that the distributions are equal
    calc
      map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => (i ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) := by sorry
      _ = map (fun ω => (j ∈ u)) (Ber(u, p)) := by sorry
      _ = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by sorry

    have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).map fun s ↦ i ∈ s) P := by
      apply HasLaw.map
      exact h₁
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).map fun s ↦ j ∈ s) P := by
      apply HasLaw.map
      exact h₁
    have h₄ : (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) = (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) := by
      have h₄₁ : i ∈ u := hi
      have h₄₂ : j ∈ u := hj
      -- Prove that the map of i is the same as the map of j by showing that the measure is invariant under element-wise bijection.
      -- Use the fact that the Bernoulli measure on product is symmetric across different elements in u.
      have h₄₃ : ∀ z : ι, z ∈ u → (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ z ∈ s)) = (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ z ∈ s)) := by
        intro z hz
        rfl
      -- Since i and j are both in u, their respective map measures are the same.
      -- More precisely, we can leverage the product structure of the Bernoulli measure to see that for each z in u, the distribution of z ∈ X is p, independently of the choice of z.
      have h₄₄ : (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) = (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) := by
        -- Use the fact that both i and j are in u to show that the measure is invariant under swapping i and j.
        have h₄₅ : ∀ (s : Set ι), i ∈ s ↔ j ∈ s := by
          intro s
          have h₄₆ := hX.1
          simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map, Set.ext_iff] at h₄₆ ⊢
          <;>
          (try contradiction) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  by_cases h : i ∈ s <;> by_cases h' : j ∈ s <;> simp_all
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      norm_num at *
                      <;>

        -- Use the fact that the map is the same to conclude that the measures are the same.

        <;>

    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) P := h₂
    have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) P := h₃
    have h₇ : (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ i ∈ s)) = (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) := h₄
    have h₈ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
      calc
        map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ i ∈ s) := by

        _ = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
          have h₉ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) (Ber(u, p).map (fun s ↦ j ∈ s)) P := h₃
          rw [h₉]
          <;> simp [HasLaw]
    exact h₈

by
    have h : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
      have h₁ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ (fun ω => X ω) ω) P := rfl
      have h₂ : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ (fun ω => X ω) ω) P := rfl
      rw [h₁, h₂]
      have h₃ : @HasLaw ι Ω _ (fun ω => X ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      have h₄ : map (fun ω => i ∈ (fun ω => X ω) ω) P = Ber(u, p) ({s : Set ι => i ∈ s}) := by
        rw [← h₃.map]
        <;> simp [measurableSet_setOf_mem]
      have h₅ : map (fun ω => j ∈ (fun ω => X ω) ω) P = Ber(u, p) ({s : Set ι => j ∈ s}) := by
        rw [← h₃.map]
        <;> simp [measurableSet_setOf_mem]
      rw [h₄, h₅]
      have h₆ : Ber(u, p) ({s : Set ι => i ∈ s}) = Ber(u, p) ({s : Set ι => j ∈ s}) := by
        -- We need to show that the measure of the sets {s : Set ι | i ∈ s} and {s : Set ι | j ∈ s} are equal under Ber(u, p)
        -- This is because i and j are in the same set u, and the Bernoulli measure is symmetric with respect to the elements of u
        -- We can use the fact that the Bernoulli measure is invariant under permutations of the elements of u
        -- to conclude that the measures of these two sets are equal
        have h₇ : ({s : Set ι => i ∈ s} : Set (Set ι)) = {s : Set ι => j ∈ s} := by
          -- We need to show that the sets {s : Set ι | i ∈ s} and {s : Set ι | j ∈ s} are the same
          -- This is because i and j are in the same set u, and the Bernoulli measure is symmetric with respect to the elements of u
          -- We can use the fact that the Bernoulli measure is invariant under permutations of the elements of u
          -- to conclude that the measures of these two sets are equal
          apply Set.ext
          intro s
          simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
          <;>
          (try simp_all) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              by_cases h₈ : i ∈ s <;> by_cases h₉ : j ∈ s <;> simp_all [h₈, h₉]
              <;>
              (try contradiction) <;>
              (try aesop)
            }
          )
          <;>
          (try
            {
              have h₁₀ : i ∈ u := hi
              have h₁₁ : j ∈ u := hj
              simp_all [h₁₀, h₁₁]
              <;>
              (try contradiction) <;>
              (try aesop)
            }
          )
        rw [h₇]
      rw [h₆]
    exact h

by
    have h1 : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
    have h2 : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P := rfl
    have h3 : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := rfl
    have h4 : (map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P) = (map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P) := by
      have h5 : Law X P = Ber(u, p) := h1.symm
      have h6 : (map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P) = (map (fun ω => i ∈ (X ω)) P) := rfl
      have h7 : (map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P) = (map (fun ω => j ∈ (X ω)) P) := rfl
      have h8 : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun s => i ∈ s) (Law X P) := by
        simp [Law, MeasureTheory.map_map, h5]
        <;>
        congr 1 <;>
        ext <;>
        simp [Function.comp]
      have h9 : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun s => j ∈ s) (Law X P) := by
        simp [Law, MeasureTheory.map_map, h5]
        <;>
        congr 1 <;>
        ext <;>
        simp [Function.comp]
      rw [h8, h9]
      have h10 : map (fun s => i ∈ s) (Ber(u, p)) = map (fun s => j ∈ s) (Ber(u, p)) := by
        have h11 : map (fun s => i ∈ s) (Ber(u, p)) = boom := by
          sorry
        have h12 : map (fun s => j ∈ s) (Ber(u, p)) = boom := by
          sorry
        rw [h11, h12]
        <;> simp
      rw [h5] at *
      exact h10
    exact h4

by
    have h₁ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := rfl
    have h₂ : map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := rfl
    have h₃ : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
    have h₄ : map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := by
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P) P :=
        by
          apply hasLaw_of_forall_ae
          intro ω
          simp
        <;> try simp_all
        <;> try norm_num
        <;> try aesop
        <;> try simp_all [HasLaw]
        <;> try aesop
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) (map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P) P :=
        by
          apply hasLaw_of_forall_ae
          intro ω
          simp
        <;> try simp_all
        <;> try norm_num
        <;> try aesop
        <;> try simp_all [HasLaw]
        <;> try aesop
      have h₇ : map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := by
        have h₈ : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := h₃
        have h₉ : (map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P : Measure (Prop)) =
            (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => i ∈ s) := by
          calc
            (map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P : Measure (Prop)) =
                P.image (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) := rfl
            _ = (map (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P).image (fun s => i ∈ s) := by
              simp [map_map]
            _ = (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => i ∈ s) := by
              have h₁₀ : HasLaw (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := by
                simpa [h₈] using h₈
              have h₁₁ : P.image (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) = Ber(u, p) := by
                apply h₁₀.map_eq
              rw [h₁₁]
              <;> simp [map_map]
        have h₁₀ : (map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P : Measure (Prop)) =
            (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
          calc
            (map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P : Measure (Prop)) =
                P.image (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) := rfl
            _ = (map (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P).image (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
              simp [map_map]
            _ = (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
              have h₁₁ : HasLaw (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) (Ber(u, p)) P := by
                simpa [h₈] using h₈
              have h₁₂ : P.image (fun ω => (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) = Ber(u, p) := by
                apply h₁₁.map_eq
              rw [h₁₂]
              <;> simp [map_map]
        have h₁₁ : (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => i ∈ s) =
            (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => j ∈ s) := by
          have h₁₂ : i ∈ u := hi
          have h₁₃ : j ∈ u := hj
          have h₁₄ : (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => i ∈ s) = p := by
            -- Use the properties of the Bernoulli distribution to show that the image of the Bernoulli measure under the map s ↦ i ∈ s is p
            sorry
          have h₁₅ : (Ber(u, p) : Measure (Set ι)).image (fun s => j ∈ s) = p := by
            -- Similarly for j
            sorry
          rw [h₁₄, h₁₅]
        rw [h₉, h₁₀, h₁₁]
      have h₁₂ : map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P = map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := h₇
      calc
        map (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω => i ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := rfl
        _ = map (fun ω => j ∈ (X : Ω → Set ι) ω) P := h₁₂
        _ = map (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P := rfl
    exact h₄

by
    have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) = (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) := rfl
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) = (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) := rfl
    -- Simplify the problem using the definition of IsBernoulliOn and properties of the product measure
    have h₃ : IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P := by
      -- Use the fact that the distribution of X is Ber(u, p) and the properties of the product measure
      have h₄ : HasLaw X (Ber(u, p)) P := hX
      -- Extract the necessary properties from the product measure to show the equality of the maps
      have h₅ : map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω))) P = Ber(u, p) := by
        simpa [HasLaw] using h₄
      -- Use the properties of the product measure to show that the maps are equal
      have h₆ : map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω i) P := by
        simp [Function.comp]
        <;>
        congr <;> ext ω <;> simp
      have h₇ : map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P := by
        simp [Function.comp]
        <;>
        congr <;> ext ω <;> simp
      -- Use the product measure properties to show the equality of the maps
      have h₈ : map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω i) P = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P := by
        -- Since i and j are in u, and the measure is symmetric, we can use this fact to prove the equality
        calc
          map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω i) P = (map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω))) P).comp (measurableEmbedding_apply i) := by
            rw [MeasurableEquiv.comp_fst_preimage]
            <;> simp [map_comp]
            <;> congr <;> ext ω <;> simp [Function.comp_apply]
          _ = Ber(u, p).comp (measurableEmbedding_apply i) := by
            rw [h₅]
          _ = Ber(u, p).comp (measurableEmbedding_apply j) := by
            -- Use the symmetry of the product measure to prove the equality
            have h₉ : i ∈ u := hi
            have h₁₀ : j ∈ u := hj
            have h₁₁ : measurableEmbedding_apply i = measurableEmbedding_apply j := by
              -- Simplify the problem using the properties of the product measure
              apply Real.instMeasurableSpaceℝ
            rw [h₁₁]
          _ = (map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω))) P).comp (measurableEmbedding_apply j) := by
            rw [h₅]
          _ = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P := by
            rw [MeasurableEquiv.comp_fst_preimage]
            <;> simp [map_comp]
            <;> congr <;> ext ω <;> simp [Function.comp_apply]
      -- Combine all the previous results to prove the final equality
      calc
        map (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω i) P := h₆
        _ = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P := h₈
        _ = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P := by
          calc
            map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P = map (fun ω ↦ (fun k ↦ (k ∈ X ω)) ω j) P := rfl
            _ = map (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P := by
              simp [Function.comp]
              <;>
              congr <;> ext ω <;> simp
    exact h₃.map_eq
